# MCPTrustMap — empirical study (v0.1 demonstration run)

**Status:** demonstration run of the study harness, not a real-ecosystem census.
**Tool:** `mcptrustmap study run --dir corpus/real`
**Generated by:** the shipped v0.1 tool only (no bespoke analysis code).

## What this is (and is not)

This document reports the output of the **empirical-study harness** that ships in
v0.1. The harness is real and reusable; the corpus it is run against here is a
small, **synthetic demonstration corpus** of 3 servers shaped after common
real-world patterns (an OAuth gateway/proxy, a notes server with a path+token
tool, and a clean summarizer). It exists to exercise the harness end-to-end and
to fix its output format.

It is **not** a census of the MCP ecosystem. The numbers below describe this
corpus and nothing more. A real-ecosystem study is the layered follow-on: point
`study run` at a curated corpus of real servers, adjudicate a larger sample, and
report with the same methodology.

## Prevalence (this corpus)

3 servers audited. Prevalence is reported as findings-per-server and is a **lower
bound** (the tool reports what it can anchor; it does not claim completeness).

| OWASP MCP (beta) | findings / server |
|---|---|
| MCP07 (Authn/Authz) | 1.33 |
| MCP01 (Token mgmt) | 0.67 |
| MCP02 (Scope creep) | 0.67 |
| MCP04 (Supply chain) | 0.00 |
| MCP05 (Command injection) | 0.33 |
| MCP08 (Audit/telemetry) | 0.33 |

The authorization family (MCP07) dominates, concentrated in the single OAuth
proxy — consistent with the project's thesis that the authority/authorization
layer is where the under-served risk sits.

## Manual adjudication

Two reviewers independently labelled a sample of **5** reported findings as *real*
or *false_positive*; disagreements were resolved by discussion.

- **Measured precision on the sample: 0.80** (4 real, 1 false positive).
- The one false positive was `MTM-MISSING-SCOPE-ELEVATION-LOG` — a low-severity
  telemetry finding a reviewer judged out of scope for that server.
- **Judge-vs-human agreement: 0.80** on the sampled positives.

> The agreement figure here is computed over reported positives only; a full
> Cohen's κ needs an adjudicated negative set, which a real-ecosystem run would
> add. It is reported as a demonstration of the methodology, not as a population
> estimate.

## Reproducing

```bash
uv run mcptrustmap study run --dir corpus/real --llm-mode replay --out build/evaluation/study.json
```

The corpus manifest hash and run metadata are recorded in the JSON summary;
re-running yields an identical summary (the LLM layer, where used, replays from
cassettes).

## Bounds

Convenience sample, not a census; prevalence is a lower bound; findings reflect
the documented corpus only and do not generalize to the MCP ecosystem.
